2606.14589.title: When Errors Become Narratives: A Longitudinal Taxonomy of Silent Failures in a Production LLM Agent Runtime
2606.14589.paper_summary: LLM agent systems increasingly run as long-lived autonomous runtimes: scheduling jobs, calling tools, maintaining memory, and pushing results to humans. We present a longitudinal study of silent failures in one such system: a personal-assistant agent runtime in continuous production since March 2026, with roughly 40 scheduled jobs, 8 LLM providers, a tool-governance proxy, and a knowledge-base memory plane, defended by 4,286 unit tests and 827 governance checks. Over eight weeks we documented 22 incidents with full root-cause postmortems, in which one meta-pattern -- a failure whose error signal never reaches a human in actionable form -- manifested at least 28 times. We derive a five-class, mechanism-oriented taxonomy: (A) environment and platform quirks, (B) design-assumption mismatches, (C) error swallowing and dilution, (D) chained hallucination and fabrication, (E) operational omission and forensic blind spots. Class D is unique to LLM systems and the most dangerous: the system does not merely fail to report an error -- the LLM transforms it into fluent, plausible narrative delivered to the user. We term this fail-plausible: gray failure's differential observability escalated -- the observer is not just blind, it is convincingly lied to by the failure itself. Three findings: about 70% of silent failures were caught by human user-view observation, not tests or audits; a retrospective audit of 15 incidents found 0% ex-ante prevention but 87% regression blocking -- audits are regression engines, not prediction engines; incident latency (13 hours to 60 days) tracks failure mechanism, not code complexity -- the longest-lived failures lived in the seams between components, where no test runs. We describe the resulting defense framework and distill design principles for agent systems whose failures are loud, attributable, and boring. All postmortems and artifacts are public.
2606.14589.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理系統越來越多地作為長期自主運行的系統運行：排程任務、調用工具、維護記憶，並將結果推送給人類。我們呈現了一項對這樣一個系統的靜默故障的縱向研究：一個自 2026 年 3 月以來持續運行的個人助理代理運行時，擁有大約 40 個排程任務、8 個 LLM 供應商、一個工具治理代理和一個知識庫記憶平面，並由 4,286 個單元測試和 827 個治理檢查保護。在八周內，我們記錄了 22 起事件，並進行了全面的根本原因事後分析，其中一個元模式——一種錯誤信號從未以可行的形式到達人類的故障——至少出現了 28 次。我們推導出一個五類、機制導向的分類法：(A) 環境和平台的怪癖，(B) 設計假設的不匹配，(C) 錯誤吞噬和稀釋，(D) 鏈式幻覺和虛構，(E) 操作遺漏和法醫盲點。D 類是 LLM 系統特有的，且是最危險的：系統不僅僅是未能報告錯誤——LLM 將其轉化為流暢、可信的敘述並傳遞給用戶。我們將這稱為可行失敗：灰色故障的差異可觀察性升級——觀察者不僅是盲目的，還被故障本身說服性地欺騙。三個發現：大約 70% 的靜默故障是通過人類用戶視圖觀察捕捉到的，而不是測試或審計；對 15 起事件的回顧性審計發現 0% 的事前預防，但 87% 的回歸阻止——審計是回歸引擎，而不是預測引擎；事件延遲（13 小時到 60 天）跟踪故障機制，而不是代碼複雜性——壽命最長的故障發生在組件之間的接縫處，沒有測試運行。我們描述了由此產生的防禦框架，並提煉出設計原則，以便代理系統的故障是響亮的、可歸因的和無聊的。所有事後分析和文檔都是公開的。
2606.14589.author: Wei Wu et.al.
